title: Mobilize | Art Inspiring Activism with Wild & Scenic Film Festival Exhibition
description: Mobilize is the first exhibition of 2026 under Art in Public Spaces and celebrates a 15-year partnership between Nevada County Arts Council and Wild & Scenic Film Festival. This year’s festival theme and title of the Rood Center exhibition, MOBILIZE, serves as a call to action and a celebration of progress toward tangible change. The exhibition explores Wild and Scenic’s rich history and its visual art exhibition, utilizing visual language to elevate voices, organize efforts, and address environmental challenges.

title: Glassblowing for Beginners – Make a Paperweight
description: Glassblowing for Beginners Level 1 – An introduction to Glassblowing, Experience the enchanting art of glassblowing and transform molten glass into a stunning physical creation in just 3 hours! This beginner-friendly class is designed to introduce you to the fundamentals of glassblowing and let you unleash your creativity., Starting with a brief safety and tool identification talk, we’ll quickly move on to the exciting part – working with hot glass! You’ll have the opportunity to gather glass, manipulate it with fire and tools, and shape it into a beautiful work of art. Under the guidance of our experienced glassblower, you’ll create a one-of-a-kind colored glass paperweight that you can proudly display in your home or office. This hands-on class will allow you to experience the magic of glassblowing while learning a new skill that you can continue to develop. Don’t miss out on this unique opportunity to explore the ancient art of glassblowing and create a beautiful piece of art to take home!, Your stunning glass creations will be ready for pickup at The Forge within a few days of your class, or you can opt for the convenience of having your masterpiece shipped to you for a small shipping and handling fee., Disclaimer: Occasionally, hot glass may break during the annealing process. If your glass piece breaks, you will have the option to select a similar replacement piece from our stock of handblown glass items., Ages 14+, Price: $180, Class minimum 2 students/maximum 4 students. If the class minimum is not met, the class will be rescheduled to a later date.

title: Nevada City Odd Fellows Open Mic
description: The most relaxed, most welcoming open mic in Western Nevada County

title: Good Morning Truckee
description: NEW!! ONLINE PRE-REGISTRATION at TruckeeChamber.com. Bring your receipt with you and breeze through the Express Check-In Line to get your breakfast and seat faster!, Hear from Jameson Stafford of Elevate Blue, Andy Scott of 1Bios and Johannes Ziegler of Tahoe Silicon Mountain on Truckee's Start-Up Scene and Tech Community.

title: African Drum and Dance of Nevada County
description: Come join our vibrant African Drum and Dance classes! Open to all ages and abilities, we explore different styles from across the African Diasporic disciplines. Including Malian, Congolese, Guinean, Senegalese, Afro-Cuban and Afro-Brazilian. Masters of their craft travel from all over to share their culture and heritage with our beautiful community, along with our own fabulous local teachers and percussionists, to offer a truly inspiring and engaging experience

title: Glassblowing Level 3 – Expand Drinking Glass Skills
description: Glassblowing Level 3 – Drinking Glass – Expand your Glassblowing Skills, Prerequisite: for those who have completed Glassblowing a Drinking Glass Level 2, Continue your Glassblowing adventures in this 4-hour workshop. Expand on your Glassworking techniques and create unique drinking cups. In this 2nd level class, you will work on perfecting your glass making skills. Students will each practice making blown glass with magnificent colored accents of their own design. You will also learn basic teamwork skills necessary to move forward to the next level of glassblowing classes., Your stunning glass creations will be ready for pickup at The Forge within a few days of your class, or you can opt for the convenience of having your masterpiece shipped to you for a small shipping and handling fee., Disclaimer: Occasionally, hot glass may break during the annealing process. If your glass piece breaks, you will have the option to select a similar replacement piece from our stock of handblown glass items., Ages 16+ and Adults, Class minimum 2 students/maximum 4 students. If the class minimum is not met, the class will be rescheduled to a later date.

title: Playwriting Group
description: Sent upon request. (We meet in each other's homes.) All playwrights welcome! Whether playwriting is new to you or you're already an accomplished playwright, come join this on-going bi-weekly writers' group. We'll spend our time focusing on our writing, but with that magical element of doing so within the presence of others. Come discover the amazing experience of writing within the context of a group. Bring your computer, notebook, iPad, or whatever you like to use for writing. We share resources, discuss our goals and challenges, even provide feedback, but the primary focus of the group is to work on your own play! We'll do a check-in/discussion at the beginning of the meeting, then write in silence, and check-out at the end of our session. Contact Kimberly for the location and to answer any questions you may have.

title: RiversLab 2: Island of a Stream
description: Join Jack Roney, SYRCL’s Americorps Restoration Coordinator, to discuss Hugeny et. al (2011) and its implications for Freshwater Conservation. Learn how the study of islands informs our understanding of isolated ecosystems everywhere, and how to harness this information to protect rare species and fragmented habitats. Highlights include: Rock-climbing fish, the native trout of Mexico, and Caribbean tidepools, RiversLab will be held at the SYRCL Office on Railroad Ave in Nevada City — a virtual option is also available., Contact Jack Roney at jack@yubariver.org for more information.

title: Karaoke - Grass Valley Brewing Co.
description: It’s Karaoke Night at GVBC with DJ Kenn!

title: Motion Theater
description: With Minica Farbiarz and Evan Specter. We have decades of exploration as performers, teachers of autobiographical, physical, embodied storytelling.

title: Tangled Roots Open Mic Slam
description: Open Mic hosted by Nevada County Poet Laureate Karen Terrey, open mic readers compete for a chance to read their work from the Main Stage at the 10th annual Sierra Poetry Festival on Saturday, April 18. Two winners will be selected by a panel of judges.

title: WinterStoke Film Festival
description: WinterStoke Film Fest is a community-driven ski and snowboard film night born out of a desire to gather the Grass Valley/Nevada City Ski and Snowboard community around shared stoke, stories, and winter culture. This year’s program features seven short films from the 2024/25 season, capturing everything from gritty, homegrown ski days and backcountry expeditions to cinematic big-mountain lines, creative snowboarding, and the humor, risk, and friendship that define life in the mountains. Together, the films celebrate not just performance, but the feel of winter—long days chasing storms, tight crews, creative expression, and the deep pull of snow-covered landscapes that connect riders across regions. More info on the films at www.winterstokefilmfest.com and @winterstokefilmfest.Trailer can be seen here: youtu.be…

title: Poetry Open Mic
description: Bring your favorite poem to share. Maybe you wrote it, maybe someone else did, but it resonated with you. Maybe you found it at just the right time ~ so feel free to share that story in a safe and welcoming place., Suggested donation $10 each meeting. No one turned away ~ Wheelchair access available., ***Please park in the rear of the Veteran's Memorial parking lot, 255 S. Auburn St, and use the path to the center.

title: Cars and coffee grass valley
description: Cars and coffee grass valley is a weekly car event at daily donuts in the Mcknight crossing shopping center every saturday morning from 7 to 10

title: FOOTHILLS CELEBRATION Step into the heart of historic downtown Grass Valley for the Foothills Celebration—an afternoon-long wine-and-food tasting adventure where every stop feels like a discovery.
description: Hosted each year by the Grass Valley Downtown Association, this beloved event invites you to stroll from venue to venue (more than 20 in total), sampling standout local and regional wines alongside chef-driven bites and specialty tastings served up by some of downtown’s most welcoming businesses. It’s part tasting tour, part downtown crawl, and all about savoring the best of the Foothills—one delicious stop at a time.

title: Watercolor Exploration with Chappy Valente
description: Immerse yourself in the expressive world of watercolor painting! Whether you’re brand new to watercolor or looking to expand your skills, this hands-on class with artist and educator Chappy Valente will help you gain confidence and technique. Learn to paint with fluidity and intention as you explore the tools, papers, and brushwork that make watercolor such a luminous and rewarding medium., Each session is a stand-alone class with fresh projects and techniques—join once or come monthly to continue growing your watercolor practice., What’s the Project:, Create your own watercolor studies exploring washes, color mixing, and texture effects. Each class introduces a new focus—such as landscapes, botanicals, or abstract forms—to inspire your creativity., What Will They Learn:, Brush control and paint-to-water ratios, Wash techniques (wet-on-wet, wet-on-dry, gradient), Paper types, brush styles, and pigment selection, Layering, and color blending, Tips for composition and adding expressive detail, Materials Needed:, All materials are provided for in-class use. Students are welcome to bring their own favorite brushes or watercolor set if desired., Skill Level / Prerequisites:, All skill levels welcome—from first-time painters to those continuing their watercolor journey., Class Length:, One 3-hour session, (Optional continuing classes offered monthly), Fee: $50 + $20 materials, Minimum 3 / Maximum 6 participants

title: Crafting Hand-Carved Wooden Spoons
description: Hand-Carved Wooden Spoons, Sunday, March 15th | 10:00 AM – 1:00 PM, There’s something deeply satisfying about shaping wood with your own two hands., In this beginner-friendly workshop, you’ll start with a simple block of wood and transform it into a beautiful, functional wooden spoon using traditional hand tools. Under expert guidance, you’ll learn how to safely and confidently use an axe, carving knife, and scoop knife to shape, hollow, and refine your piece., This class is perfect for anyone curious about traditional woodworking or wanting to slow down and experience the rhythm of working with hand tools. No prior experience is necessary — just bring your curiosity and a willingness to learn. By the end of class, you’ll leave with a hand-carved wooden spoon and the foundational skills to continue carving on your own., What You’ll Create , One hand-carved wooden spoon, What You’ll Learn, • Safe and proper use of an axe, • Knife carving techniques, • How to use a scoop knife for hollowing, • Foundational greenwood carving skills, Experience Level, Beginner — No experience needed, Ages- Adults, and Teens 13+ may participate when accompanied by an adult. (The accompanying adult may choose to observe or purchase a ticket to participate.), All materials and tools are provided.

title: Wool Party at the Mill
venue_name: Siren Song Fleece Works at Lucchesi Vineyards
description: Celebrate the beginning of shearing season with us! Get your hands in some fresh wool, learn the art of skirting a fleece, and help out with fiber prep in the mill. Bring your own fleece or play with ours.

title: Frisson presented by InConcert Sierra
description: FRISSON, March 15, 2026 ∙ 2pm, with 1:15 pre-concert forum, Frisson is a dynamic chamber ensemble based in New York City, celebrated for its vibrant performances and innovative programming. Under the artistic direction of oboist Thomas Gallant, the group features a rotating roster of exceptional young musicians, many of whom are alumni of prestigious institutions like Juilliard, Yale, and the Manhattan School of Music ., The ensemble's name, "Frisson," meaning "a shiver or thrill" in French, encapsulates their mission to deliver exhilarating musical experiences. Their repertoire spans a wide range, from classical masterworks to contemporary compositions, often arranged for unique instrument combinations. Frisson's flexible ensemble structure allows them to perform in various configurations, including quintets, sextets, nonets, and even a small chamber orchestra ., They will include Prokofiev's "Peter and the Wolf" narrated by our production manager Micah Cone, who is also a noted local actor, voted the "Best Local Performer" of 2024!

title: Glenn Miller Orchestra
description: The most popular and sought after big band in the world today for both concert and swing dance engagements. With its unique jazz sound, the Glenn Miller Orchestra is considered to be one of the greatest bands of all time. The present Glenn Miller Orchestra was formed in 1956 and has been touring worldwide ever since.

title: Fogelberg Revisited – A Tribute to Dan Fogelberg
description: Celebrate timeless songwriting at the historic Miners Foundry Cultural Center with Fogelberg Revisited, a heartfelt tribute to Dan Fogelberg. Produced and performed by Paul Michael Costa, the show features special guest performances by Kimberly Bass and Sheldon Felich, with opening guests The Dolli Mamas. Doors 3 PM | Show 4 PM , A no-host full bar and refreshments will be available , Bring your printed ticket or QR code for entry , Expect memorable performances, inspired arrangements, and a warm community atmosphere in historic downtown Nevada City. Presented in loving memory of friend and musical partner Paul Godwin.

title: The Rules of the Game (La Regle du jeu) (1939)
description: THE RULES OF THE GAME is a scathing critique of corrupt French society cloaked in a comedy of manners, in which a weekend at a marquis’s country château lays bare some ugly truths about a group of haut-bourgeois acquaintances. The film has had a tumultuous history: it was subjected to cuts after the violent response of the audience at its 1939 premiere, and the original negative was destroyed during World War II; it wasn’t reconstructed until 1959. That version, which has stunned viewers for decades, will be presented at the Onyx Downtown on March 15th! IN FRENCH W/ ENGLISH SUBTITLES

title: Tuesday Morning Ceramics – Hand-Building Techniques
description: Tuesday Morning Ceramics and Pottery Class with Pamela Montalbano, 6 class series, Tuesday Mornings 10:30 am – 12:30 pm, Price: $271 Public ($216 for 6 classes + $55 materials), Discover your creativity, engage your mind, and come play with clay! In this Ceramics and Pottery Class, we will use hand-building techniques to make functional and non-functional ceramics and pottery projects using slabs and coils. Clay, Glazing, and firings are included in the materials fee. Participants can sign up for additional Tuesday Morning classes each month if they wish to continue their ceramics adventure., We will be hand-building and then every third class will be a glazing class. At least 2 projects will be made in this series., Class 5 minimum/8 maximum participants – (please note: if attendance does not meet the minimum, the class will be rescheduled)

title: African Drum and Dance of Nevada County
description: Come join our vibrant African Drum and Dance classes! Open to all ages and abilities, we explore different styles from across the African Diasporic disciplines. Including Malian, Congolese, Guinean, Senegalese, Afro-Cuban and Afro-Brazilian. Masters of their craft travel from all over to share their culture and heritage with our beautiful community, along with our own fabulous local teachers and percussionists, to offer a truly inspiring and engaging experience

title: Cosplay Curious: Teen Mixed Media Art Club – New Date 3/17
description: Cosplay Curious: Teen Mixed Media Art Club and Creative Get Together, A bi-monthly youth art group at The Curious Forge, Come as you are. Make something bold. Meet other creative teens., This bi-monthly Teen Art Club is a welcoming, inclusive creative meetup for youth ages 14–19 who are interested in art, cosplay, mixed media, and hands-on making. Facilitated by artist Clara Frawley, this group blends art-making, skill-sharing, and community connection in a relaxed, supportive environment., Each bi-monthly meetup will blend creative warm-ups, hands-on making, and skill-building in a relaxed, social studio environment. Participants will explore drawing, design, pattern-making, and mixed-media techniques while developing personal and collaborative projects. Sessions may include playful identity-based art, wearable or sculptural forms, safe tool and material use, and opportunities for skill sharing. As the group grows, projects will evolve based on shared interests and creative curiosity., No prior experience required—just curiosity and a willingness to try something new. We take great care to create a welcoming and inclusive environment where every person can feel valued and supported., Reserve your space with Free Ticket so we know to expect you. , $10 donation recommended. No one will be turned away for lack of funds.

title: Spring Street Swing Out
venue_name: Odd Fellows Hall, 212 Spring Street, Nevada City CA 95959
description: 🎶 Get Ready to Swing the Night Away! 💃🕺, Join us for our monthly Swing Dance & Lindy Hop Night – a high-energy evening filled with music, movement, and community fun!, 🕕 Intro Lesson: 6–7 PM – Perfect for beginners! No experience or partner needed., 🎉 Social Dance to follow – Dance the night away with fellow swing lovers!, 💵 Tickets:, • $10 – DJ Nights (6pm-9pm), • $15 – Live Band Nights (6pm-10pm), 🎓 Students: $5 off with valid ID!, Beer and Nevada City Winery wine available for purchase., 🎷Check our calendar for upcoming live band nights and special events:, 👉 nevadacountyswings.org, Whether you're stepping onto the dance floor for the first time or you're a Lindy pro, there's a spot for you. See you there!

title: Rashomon Dinner Movie Fundraiser
description: As a prelude to the upcoming stage production of RASHOMON, Community Asian Theatre of the Sierra (CATS) and The Onyx Downtown are co-presenting a special screening of Akira Kurosawa’s acclaimed 1950 film at the Nevada Theatre, starring Toshirô Mifune. This fundraiser for CATS is presented as a “Bento Box Dinner and Movie.” Guests will have the option to purchase a bento box dinner from Kaliko’s Hawaiian Kitchen food truck, parked outside the Nevada Theatre, and enjoy their meal inside the theatre. Before the screening, there will be a Japanese sword fight demonstration. Following the film, Sonny Alforque, director of the stage production, will lead a brief talk exploring how Kurosawa’s film is interpreted and reimagined for the stage. This special screening offers a thoughtful introduction to the live theatrical performance of RASHOMON, which runs April 23 – May 16 at the Nevada Theatre. Tickets at www.theonyxtheatre.com.

title: Rashomon Dinner Movie Fundraiser for CATS
description: As a prelude to the upcoming stage production of RASHOMON, Community Asian Theatre of the Sierra (CATS) and The Onyx Downtown are co-presenting a special screening of Akira Kurosawa’s acclaimed 1950 film at the Nevada Theatre, starring Toshirô Mifune. This fundraiser for CATS is presented as a “Bento Box Dinner and Movie.” Guests will have the option to purchase a bento box from Kaliko’s Hawaiian Kitchen food truck, parked outside the Nevada Theatre, and enjoy their meal inside the theatre. Before the screening, there will be a Japanese sword fight demonstration. Following the film, Sonny Alforque, director of the stage production, will lead a brief talk exploring how Kurosawa’s film is interpreted and reimagined for the stage. This special screening offers a thoughtful introduction to the live theatrical performance of RASHOMON, which runs April 23 – May 16 at the Nevada Theatre. Tickets at www.theonyxtheatre.com.

title: The Gipsy Kings ft. Tonino Baliardo
description: Tonino Baliardo is the founder and leader of Gipsy Kings, but above all he is a guitar virtuoso and a composer recognized worldwide. He composed and produced the greatest hits of the group such as “Bamboleo”or “Djobi Djoba”.Undisputed masters of gypsy music, the group has sold over 20 million albums worldwide and has been awarded numerous gold, platinum and diamond discs, a music victory in 1990 and a Grammy Award in 2013 in the Best World Music category for the album “Savor Flamenco”. He also collaborated on the film “The Big Lebowski”by remixing “Hotel California”, and on “Toy Story III”, the Disney Pixar animated film with “You’ve Got a Friend in Me”.Most recently Tonino was awarded a platinum record for his duet with Takagi Ketra and a triple platinum record with “Ingobernable”in a duet with Tangana.He has performed in the most prestigious concert halls around the world: Radio City in New York, the Hollywood Bowl or the Greek Theater in Los Angeles, but also the Royal Albert Hall in London or the Olympia in Paris. Tonino said in an interview “We started in Saint Tropez with our guitars and since then, we have travelled around the world. Music has no borders and our music is universal”. This must be the secret to 30 years of success: an eternity in the world of music. If the story written by the Gipsy Kings and Tonino Baliardo draws its sources from the gypsy tradition, it is resolutely geared towards the future and targets all generations.Family and transmission are essential values in the eyes of Tonino who has been accompanied by his two sons Mikaël and Cosso Baliardo since 2014.Today, he continues to travel the world to share a music which is unique to him.

title: Meet the Author!
description: Take Up Space -, A guide to living creatively, vibrantly, and un-apologetically in your second act., What if your most creative, passionate, fulfilling years - are just beginning?, You are invited to meet the author!,

title: Stained Glass Curved Cuts- Level 2
description: Stained Glass. Level 2 – Curved Cuts, Prerequisite: Intro to Stained Glass Level 1 or equivalent stained glass class at The Curious Forge, Take your stained glass skills to the next level! This Level 2 class focuses on mastering curved and inside cuts using the techniques learned in Level 1. You’ll refine your cutting, shaping, and assembly skills while creating a beautiful curved suncatcher approximately 5″ in diameter. Students must be comfortable making straight cuts before taking this class, A materials cost of $20 is required on the day. Payment to the facilitator can be made using cash or Venmo., What You’ll Learn:, How to confidently cut inside and outside curves, Techniques for grinding and fitting curved glass pieces, Tips for designing and laying out curved patterns, Review soldering techniques for curved seams, How to assemble and finish a multi-piece curved glass project, Details:, Duration: 3 hours, Price: $79 class fee + plus additional material fee $20 is required during class. Payment to the facilitator can be made using cash or Venmo., Ages: Adults and 16+, Participants: Minimum of 3, maximum of 6 (Class will be rescheduled if minimum attendance is not met) Please note: All classes require a minimum number of participants and may be rescheduled if that minimum is not met. If this happens, we’ll let you know in advance and work with you to get you into the next available class., Join us for this creative journey and leave with your own stunning stained glass suncatcher!

title: Needle Felting Creations: Mingle & Make
description: Mingle & Make – Needle Felting Creations, Unwind, get creative, and enjoy a relaxed evening of making at our Mingle & Make: Needle Felting Creations class! Whether you’re brand new to needle felting or a seasoned crafter, this social, hands-on workshop invites you to explore color, texture, and form while creating your own whimsical felted project to take home. Come solo or bring a friend to share the fun—snacks and beverages included! Bring a friend and receive a $5 off coupon toward your next class., Price: $35 + $10 materials fee per person, No experience necessary, Class 6 minimum/11 maximum participants – (please note: if attendance does not meet minimum, class will be rescheduled)

All ages welcome., Learn more at thecenterforthearts.org

title: Decendants: The Musical
description: Astonishing Theater Company presents Descendants: The Musical, a high-energy stage production inspired by the popular Disney film series. Follow the children of famous villains as they leave the Isle of the Lost and enter the kingdom of Auradon, where they must decide who they want to become. Featuring dynamic choreography, familiar songs, and a vibrant young cast, the production explores identity, friendship, and the power of choosing your own path. Performances take place at First Baptist Church Grass Valley with evening and weekend shows available.

description: Develop your jazz improvisation skills with a wonderful teacher and bassist, Andrew Mell. All instruments, voices and levels are welcome. Andrew, who plays and teaches in Sacramento and at the University of the Pacific, is warm and welcoming and a great teacher.

title: Saturday Get Behind the Wheel Ceramics
description: Get Behind A Wheel – Pottery Wheel Class for beginners and advanced students with Pam Montalbano, 6 class series – Saturday Mornings, 10:30 am – 12:30 pm, This workshop is for all levels (beginners welcome) who wish to develop and sharpen their pottery-throwing skills on the Potter’s Wheel. We will go over the basics of wedging, centering, pulling, and trimming but also cover how to throw bowls and plates. Everyone is guaranteed to leap a pot. Make that special pot that shows the sudden uptick in your skills. Enjoy playing with clay and making ceramic pottery with your own unique flair. All clay and materials included., Ages: 16+, Price: $216 + $55 materials PUBLIC = $271, Class 4 minimum/8 maximum participants – (please note: if attendance does not meet the minimum, the class will be rescheduled), Pre-registration required.

title: UNESCO World Poetry Day
description: World Poetry Day is a celebration highlighting poetry's role in promoting harmony and social cohesion, particularly by amplifying marginalized voices and supporting endangered languages., Join us for a generative afternoon and write a poem or two. Your poems will be on display during Sierra Poetry Festival on Saturday, April 18 at The Center for the Arts in Grass Valley.

description: Join in a magical sound experience created from crystal singing bowls, voice and other instruments. Your personally requested blessing will be infused into the sound. Lay back, relax and float on the vibrations.

title: Dirty Cello: High-Voltage Strings at Church Street
description: Dirty Cello brings its high-energy, genre-defying sound to the Church Street Concert Series on Saturday, March 21, 2026 at 7:00 PM, performing live at the historic Grass Valley United Methodist Church. Led by dynamic cellist Rebecca Roudman, Dirty Cello transforms the cello into a powerful lead instrument, blending blues, rock, Americana, bluegrass, and roots music into an exciting and soulful live experience. Known for their infectious stage presence and musical versatility, the band delivers performances that are engaging, joyful, and full of surprises, appealing to audiences of all ages and musical tastes. , This concert offers a rare opportunity to hear world-class musicianship in an intimate and welcoming setting. The Church Street Concert Series is celebrated for pairing outstanding artists with a space where sound, community, and connection come together. Dirty Cello’s music ranges from foot-stomping grooves to expressive, emotional passages, creating a night that invites listeners to tap their feet, smile, and fully immerse themselves in the moment.

title: Decendants: The Musical
description: Astonishing Theater Company presents Descendants: The Musical, a high-energy stage production inspired by the popular Disney film series. Follow the children of famous villains as they leave the Isle of the Lost and enter the kingdom of Auradon, where they must decide who they want to become. Featuring dynamic choreography, familiar songs, and a vibrant young cast, the production explores identity, friendship, and the power of choosing your own path. Performances take place at First Baptist Church Grass Valley with evening and weekend shows available.

title: Sounds from Darkness and Light: Enacted Poetry and Music
description: Sounds from Darkness and Light is enacted poetry drawn from Una Kobrin's collection Words Between Darkness and Light, with Micah Cone and friends offering music that breathes through the poems, creating something that is neither quite a reading nor a concert, but a weaving of both in an inner journey moving through a range of — levity, wrestlings and wonder. Complimentary refreshments.

title: Leila and the Wolves
description: 3/22 - A film unlike any other, Heiny Srour’s LEILA AND THE WOLVES draws on the Arab heritage of oral tradition and mosaic pattern to combine fictional drama, archival footage, and fantasy sequences that explore the collective memory of Arab women and their hidden role in history throughout the past half-century in Palestine and Lebanon. This anti-colonial, narrative-documentary was filmed over seven years with women from Lebanon and Palestine, capturing the intersectionality of the struggles women faced in occupied Palestine. The character of Leila (Nabila Zeitouni) travels through time, from the British Mandate of Palestine to the Israeli invasion of Lebanon in 1982, to blend memory and myth. IN ARABIC W/ ENGLISH SUBTITLES
